ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or Another Status Report And Petition
Off Calendar Dec-12-2018 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-20-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Vivian Holley for Petitioner.

The Court questions whether it is appropriate to charge the Estate for all of the work done with the marriage when in the Court's view there was an initial incorrect view of the law which required court appearances and an amended petition. Ms. Holley stated that she has reduced fees by 40%, that there is no charge for some of the hearings and the additional pleadings, and that an asterisk at the end of any entry means that the line was reduced or there was no charge. Ms. Holley also stated that the marriage hours were 72.65 for Paralegal and Attorney time, that 17.75 hours were no charge, that she tried to categorize the charges (marriage/sale/ancillary), that with the reduction the average rate was $215.00 and that conversations with Jessie Reed's family generally were not charged. The First and Final Account, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Closing Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-12-2017 Continued To Sep-26-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions Re: The Validity Of Decedents Alleged Marriage To Jessie Hamilton (Probate Code 9611 And Family Code 2201)
Off Calendar Jan-17-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Blackman for Theresa Armstard (appeared by telephone).
Torieno Brown, Jackie Hamilton and Nyesha Hamilton also present.

The parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the issue of whether or not the marriage of Harvey Reed and Jessie Hamilton is/is not valid. The Petition for Instructions Re: the Validity of Decedents Alleged Marriage to Jessie Hamilton is taken under submission. Mr. Blackman informed the Court that he filed a notice of unavailability.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported.

Petition For Instructions Re: The Validity Of Decedents Alleged Marriage To Jessie Hamilton (Probate Code 9611 And Family Code 2201)
Off Calendar Dec-13-2016 Continued To Jan-10-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions Re: The Validity Of Decedents Alleged Marriage To Jessie Hamilton (Probate Code 9611 And Family Code 2201)
Off Calendar Sep-27-2016 Continued To Dec-13-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Blackman for (administrator) Theresa Armstard. Jacqueline Hamilton, Eric Hamilton, Torieno Brown & Nyesha Hamilton (objectors) in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that we are here on a Petition for Instructions, confirmed that the Hamilton's were present to object, stated that objections need to be filed in writing explaining their position and strongly suggested that they obtain an attorney to know what their rights are and what they can and cannot do. Mr. Blackman asked if it was possible to appoint a pro bono attorney, that there is only $140,000.00 in the estate and that he doesn't want it eaten up. The Court responded that it cannot appoint an attorney, that if the parties wish to continue to object they must file their objections no later than November 30, 2016 so that the Court and Mr. Blackman will have an opportunity to see what the objections are and suggested that the parties talk to each other to try and resolve the issues. The Petition for Instructions Re: The Validity of Decedents Alleged Marriage to Jessie Hamilton was continued to December 13, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not reported)

Petition For Instructions Re: The Validity Of Decedents Alleged Marriage To Jessie Hamilton (Probate Code 9611 And Family Code 2201)
Off Calendar Aug-02-2016 Continued To Sep-27-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Blackman for petitioner Theresa Armstard.

The Court advised counsel that the legal authority he had cited to the Court regarding the appropriate standard to be applied had been rejected and was not the appropriate standard that needed to be applied in this matter. Mr. Blackman stated that the objective fact was that Jessie had told him she was the decedent's wife. The Court responded that he was not going to make any factual determination at today's hearing, and directed that the petitions be served upon the decedent's heirs. The matter was continued to September 27, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not reported)
